name: Memory Interface & Timing
description: Sub-sector template for memory interface patents (CPC G11C7*, G11C8*) covering access timing, addressing, bus protocols, and I/O circuits.
contextDescription: Memory interface patents cover the critical path between processors and memory. Key areas include I/O buffers, timing calibration, signal integrity (DQ/DQS training), command/address decoding, and memory bus protocols. Every DDR, HBM, LPDDR, and GDDR interface relies on these circuits. High-value targets include memory vendors (Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron), controller IP vendors (Synopsys, Cadence), and all SoC/processor vendors (Intel, AMD, Apple, Qualcomm) that implement memory controllers.
scoringGuidance: DDR timing calibration patents read on every processor and memory chip.
Signal integrity (write leveling, read training) patents are mandatory for DDR4/5.
High-bandwidth memory (HBM) interface patents target AI accelerator and GPU vendors.
LPDDR interface patents target all mobile SoC vendors.
Memory controller patents are broader than memory-side patents — they read on processor vendors.

questions.question: What memory interface standard does this patent relate to? Score by market volume: 1-3 for legacy/niche, 4-6 for current-gen (DDR4, LPDDR4), 7-10 for next-gen or multi-standard (DDR5, HBM, LPDDR5).
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify the memory interface standard and its current market relevance.

questions.question: Does this patent cover timing calibration, training, or signal integrity (write leveling, DQ training, DBI)? These are mandatory techniques that every implementation must use.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Mandatory training sequences are very hard to design around. Assess if this is required by the standard.

questions.question: Does this patent cover the memory controller side (reads on processor/SoC vendors) or the memory PHY/device side (reads on DRAM vendors), or both? Controller-side patents have more potential targets.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify whether this targets the controller, PHY, or memory device side.

questions.question: Does this patent improve memory bandwidth, latency, or power efficiency? These are the key competitive metrics for memory interfaces.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Identify specific bandwidth, latency, or power improvements.
